explicit_text: I was born in Lithuania and came to the United States at age nine in 1933 during the Depression. My family spoke several languages including Lithuanian, and we left just before the worst of the Nazi persecution. Sadly, my uncle Herschel and his family were victims of the Holocaust in Lithuania, with my uncle dying at Dachau after being experimented on by Dr. Mengele.

entity: European Theater of World War II
explicit_text: I served in the European Theater of World War II for about eleven years, more than most troops. I was part of the 29th Engineer Combat Battalion, involved in combat engineering tasks like building and destroying bridges. My service took me through England, Scotland, and eventually into Nazi-occupied Europe.
